mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS > 130,000 and UNRATE < 8.0 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding real economic activity that increases corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to produce positive 60d returns in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO > 95.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes that increase equity discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL > 2.5 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) during a demand-driven expansion signals sustained consumer spending momentum that lifts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to produce positive 60d returns in 50%+ of instances during periods of sustained HOUST growth.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) reflects weakening supply-side capacity utilization that reduces corporate profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO < 95 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent low inflation (CPIAUCSL < 2%) and low policy rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%) indicate accommodative monetary conditions that support equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL < 2.0 and FEDFUNDS < 1.0 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 8.0% signals tighter labor markets that increase wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 8.0 despite the signal.
